summary: The first-gen BRZ is a simple, naturally aspirated sports car with excellent reliability. The FA20 engine has a known valve spring recall and some oil consumption concerns, but is otherwise robust.

issues.name: Valve Spring Recall
issues.description: Certain 2013 BRZ models had a valve spring recall due to springs that could fracture and damage the engine. Verify recall has been completed.

issues.name: Oil Consumption
issues.description: The FA20 engine can consume oil at elevated rates, particularly during high-RPM driving. Subaru considers up to 1 quart per 1,200 miles acceptable.

issues.name: Throwout Bearing Noise
issues.description: The throwout bearing chirps when the clutch pedal is released. A common Subaru trait that is annoying but not harmful in the short term.

issues.name: Direct Injection Carbon Buildup
issues.description: The direct injection FA20 can develop carbon buildup on the intake valves, causing rough idle and power loss over time. Walnut blasting cleans it.

issues.name: Weak Stock Clutch
issues.description: The factory clutch is light-duty and can slip under spirited driving, especially on cars with exhaust and tune modifications.

buyingTips: Verify the valve spring recall status on 2013 models — this is the most critical check
Check oil level during test drive — the FA20 can consume oil, especially when driven hard at high RPM
The BRZ is a simple car mechanically — there are few things that go wrong compared to turbocharged competitors
Listen for throwout bearing chirp with the clutch pedal out — it is common but not dangerous
2017+ models received minor improvements (revised suspension, updated infotainment) and are preferred
